name: mild_day_planned_window_no_pause
description: Mild day with windows_recommended=True and planned window 10:00–17:00. Outdoor is cooler than indoor. Sensor opens at 12:00 (inside the window). The planned-window gate fires — no_action (not a pause). If outdoor was below threshold, nat-vent would normally activate, but the gate takes priority and blocks even nat-vent evaluation.
notes: The planned-window gate at position 3 fires BEFORE nat-vent evaluation when within window.
So even with favorable nat-vent conditions, no_action is produced (not natural_ventilation).
This is correct: the planned window period tells CA to stay out of the way entirely.
outdoor=65F < indoor=73F, indoor=73F > comfort_heat=70F, outdoor < threshold — nat-vent conditions met,
but gate takes priority over nat-vent evaluation.
Covers Priority 6 mild_day_planned_window from plan abundant-mapping-clarke.md (C5×E1 cell).

events.note: Late morning: indoor 73F, outdoor 65F — nat-vent conditions would be favorable
Window opens at 12:00 inside the 10:00–17:00 planned window — gate should suppress (no_action)

assertions.reason: Mild-day classification applied: hvac_mode=off, windows_recommended=True, 10:00–17:00 window
sensor_open during planned window period — gate fires before nat-vent check, no_action produced even though outdoor < indoor and nat-vent conditions are favorable

verdict.summary: Planned window gate suppresses sensor_open response on mild day — even when nat-vent conditions are met
verdict.observed_behavior: classification_applied (off, windows recommended) → sensor_open at 12:00 → no_action (planned window gate)
verdict.expected_behavior: Sensor open inside planned window suppressed entirely; CA stays hands-off during window period
